meta.title: Maths Flashcards – Measures & Units
meta.description: Interactive flashcards for measures, units and conversions with visual guides and real-world examples
meta.author: Kharadi Enterprise

cards.question: How many millimetres in 1 centimetre?
cards.answer: 10 mm. Visual: Think of a standard pencil eraser – it's about 1 cm long, which equals 10 mm. The prefix 'milli-' means thousandth, so there are 1000 mm in a metre, and 100 cm in a metre, giving 10 mm per cm.

cards.question: How many centimetres in 1 metre?
cards.answer: 100 cm. Visual: A standard ruler is 30 cm. Three rulers end-to-end would be close to 1 metre. The prefix 'centi-' means hundredth, so 100 cm = 1 m.

cards.question: How many metres in 1 kilometre?
cards.answer: 1000 m. Visual: A kilometre is roughly the distance you can walk in 10-12 minutes at a normal pace. The prefix 'kilo-' means thousand, so 1 km = 1000 m.

cards.question: How many millilitres in 1 litre?
cards.answer: 1000 ml. Visual: A standard water bottle holds about 500 ml, so two bottles make 1 litre. A large jug of milk is typically 1 litre.

cards.question: How many centilitres in 1 litre?
cards.answer: 100 cl. Visual: Wine bottles are often labelled in cl – a standard wine bottle is 75 cl (750 ml). The prefix 'centi-' means hundredth, so 100 cl = 1 litre.

cards.question: How many millilitres in 1 centilitre?
cards.answer: 10 ml. Visual: A teaspoon holds about 5 ml, so 2 teaspoons = 10 ml = 1 cl. This is useful for measuring small amounts of liquid in cooking.

cards.question: How many grams in 1 kilogram?
cards.answer: 1000 g. Visual: A standard bag of sugar is 1 kg. A bar of chocolate is often 100 g, so 10 chocolate bars would weigh 1 kg.

cards.question: How many milligrams in 1 gram?
cards.answer: 1000 mg. Visual: A paperclip weighs about 1 gram. Medication doses are often measured in mg – 500 mg is half a gram.

cards.question: How many seconds in 1 minute?
cards.answer: 60 seconds. Visual: The second hand on an analogue clock takes exactly 60 seconds to complete one full circle.

cards.question: How many minutes in 1 hour?
cards.answer: 60 minutes. Visual: The minute hand on a clock takes 60 minutes to go from 12 back to 12. Half an hour is 30 minutes.

cards.question: How many hours in 1 day?
cards.answer: 24 hours. Visual: From midnight to midnight is 24 hours. Half a day is 12 hours.

cards.question: How many days in 1 week?
cards.answer: 7 days. The days are: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday. A school week is typically 5 days.

cards.question: How many weeks in 1 year?
cards.answer: 52 weeks. Since there are 365 days in a year and 7 days in a week, 365 ÷ 7 = 52 weeks and 1 day (or 2 days in a leap year).

cards.question: How many days in a common year?
cards.answer: 365 days. A common year has 12 months: 7 months have 31 days, 4 months have 30 days, and February has 28 days.

cards.question: How many days in a leap year?
cards.answer: 366 days. Leap years occur every 4 years (years divisible by 4), when February has 29 days instead of 28. Example: 2024, 2028, 2032.

cards.question: How many months in 1 year?
cards.answer: 12 months. The months are: January, February, March, April, May, June, July, August, September, October, November, December.

cards.question: How many seconds in 1 hour?
cards.answer: 3600 seconds. Calculation: 60 seconds × 60 minutes = 3600 seconds. This is useful for timing events or calculating speeds.

cards.question: How many millimetres in 1 metre?
cards.answer: 1000 mm. Visual: Since there are 100 cm in a metre and 10 mm in each cm, 100 × 10 = 1000 mm. A standard door is about 2000 mm (2 m) tall.

cards.question: How many centimetres in 1 kilometre?
cards.answer: 100,000 cm. Calculation: 1 km = 1000 m, and 1 m = 100 cm, so 1000 × 100 = 100,000 cm. This shows how large a kilometre is compared to centimetres.

cards.question: What is the unit of length in the metric system?
cards.answer: The metre (m). It is the base unit of length in the International System of Units (SI). All other length units (km, cm, mm) are derived from it.

cards.question: What is the unit of mass in the metric system?
cards.answer: The gram (g). The kilogram (kg) is the base SI unit (1000 g = 1 kg). Mass measures how much matter is in an object, different from weight.

cards.question: What is the unit of capacity/volume in the metric system?
cards.answer: The litre (L or l). 1 litre = 1000 ml = 1000 cubic centimetres (cm³). A cube with sides of 10 cm holds exactly 1 litre of water.

cards.question: How do you convert between metric units using prefixes?
cards.answer: Remember: kilo- (×1000), hecto- (×100), deca- (×10), base unit, deci- (÷10), centi- (÷100), milli- (÷1000). Moving down the scale multiply, moving up divide.

cards.question: What is the relationship between grams, millilitres, and water?
cards.answer: 1 ml of water weighs approximately 1 gram at room temperature. This means 1 litre of water weighs about 1 kg. This is very useful for cooking and science.

cards.question: How do you remember the metric conversion order?
cards.answer: Use the mnemonic 'King Henry Died By Drinking Chocolate Milk': Kilo, Hecto, Deca, Base, Deci, Centi, Milli. Each step is ×10 or ÷10.
